mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly self-hosted AI platform designed for seamless integration with various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It is ideal for users looking for an extensible and feature-rich AI deployment solution that operates entirely offline. Key trade-offs include the need for self-hosting and management of infrastructure.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a Kubernetes cluster with the desired version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Deploy Open WebUI
steps.description:
Install Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> This command installs Open WebUI in the specified namespace.
commands: helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the deployed pods to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> This command lists all pods in the Open WebUI namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment open-webui --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 -n open-webui

```

> This command sets up autoscaling based on CPU usage.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI from the cluster. This will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, perform the upgrade, and verify the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating deployment: failed to create deployment'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured service accounts.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account has the necessary permissions to create deployments.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Kubernetes cannot pull the specified container image.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the image is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens when the application inside the pod fails to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n open-webui' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the request to the Kubernetes API server timed out.

**Fix:**
Increase the timeout settings or check the network connectivity to the API server.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama model integration and improved API compatibility.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Open WebUI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n open-webui'.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui
gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
